# Transcrição: 05. Consistency of What

- **Fonte:** 05. Consistency of What
- **Duração:** 04:14
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Thomas Edison said, vision without execution is hallucination. In other words, it's not enough just to put our why and hows into words. We must put them into action. They are tools to focus our energy and seize those opportunities that are in tune with what it is we believe. So how does this work? Let me explain with a metaphor. Let's say that you're at a party and people are giving you advice on the best snacks to buy your team for a long day in the office. Some say potato chips, others say fruit and veggie platters, while others point you toward chocolate and candy. So you go to the supermarket with all this good advice from all these people and you buy everything. You buy potato chips, fruit and veggie platters, and all kinds of chocolate and candy. You spend a lot of time in the supermarket and you spend a lot of money. And you may or may not get value out of some or all of these products. And the worst part is, when you're standing in the checkout line with all these snacks in your cart, no one can see what you believe because you bought everything. Now let's imagine you get all the same advice from all the same people that this time you know your why. To care for the health and well-being of everyone so that we can live life to the fullest. Now what do you buy? This time you only buy the fruit and veggie platters. They are the only choices that make sense. So when you're standing in the checkout line with only fruit and veggies in your cart, people can see what you believe. Someone walking past could say, hey, I see you believe in being healthy. So do I. You just attracted someone who believes what you believe simply because what you do reflects what you believe. And here's the neatest part. As soon as I said the why, you immediately knew what I was going to buy. The power of our why is that it helps us choose the what's that just act as proof of our why and people can quickly see what we believe. That's called scale because it quickly gives us the potential to rapidly attract larger numbers of people who share similar values. We don't even have to explain anything. Simply by saying and doing things that are lying with our why, people will see what we believe. And then two things happen. First, we feel more fulfilled by what we do. And second, it quickly builds trust with those around us because they can see that our actions are consistent and authentic, true to what we say we believe. And that's really the goal. Now let's build on this a little and look at our everyday life. Many of us spend more time at work than we do at home. Ideally, the careers we choose and the organizations we work for align with who we are. However our situation, we are at our natural best and feel fulfilled when we surround ourselves with people who believe what we believe. As we well know, sometimes things don't feel perfect.

## [03:01 - 04:14]

Sometimes we find ourselves in a place where we feel like we don't belong or where we're not thriving. The cause of this feeling could be our environment or it could be how we're showing up in our environment. When we feel this way, equipped with our why and hows, this is actually a great opportunity to take a step back and use them as filters to look at our situation. So for example, one of my house is get up on the balcony. In other words, I'm pretty good at seeing the big picture. But sometimes that can slow me down because I'm thinking of all the different implications of a single choice rather than just getting on with it. As another example, I'll draw on my why, which is all about propelling people forward. If I'm working with a team or a person who's not interested in progress or positive change, I'm going to be really frustrated. So I'd actually be better off working with someone who is eager to make progress. So ask yourself, how could I be more in tune with my why and hows? Once you've put your finger on something, try to articulate what may be contributing to the unsettled feeling you're having. Take some time to reflect and use your why and hows to truly understand what's going on.
